what_it_does: Reads projection and prompt registries, builds a JSON schema for structured output, surveys file forms, then for each target file either skips LLM rendering if the form is classified as light or renders legibility projections via a local model (chunking large files with overlap), merges chunk outputs, and writes the results plus code-derived format, lineage, links, and blocks into an SQLite ledger in a resume-safe, single-writer, per-file-commit manner.
observations: Hardcoded ROOT path points to a Windows/WSL directory under /mnt/c/Users/Workstation001/Documents/Claude/Projects/Visual Designer.
CHUNK_CHARS=85000 and OVERLAP=10000 define sliding-window chunking for files exceeding the threshold.
Uses fleet.local4b with json_schema=SCHEMA and max_tokens=4000 to produce constrained JSON.
Light-form heuristic skips model rendering when the form name contains log, status, chronicle, index, or moc.
Merging chunked outputs deduplicates arrays and selects the longest scalar string by length.
run() is resume-safe: it skips rels already present in files with substance=0 or a non-empty principle.
Writes call_log entries from fleet.CALL_LOG after each completed future, then clears the log.
The __main__ block samples up to two files per form from carve.json unless --full is passed.
An unused _old_write function remains in the file.
Database schema migration and column additions are applied idempotently inside __main__.
standouts: Chunked LLM rendering with overlap and a custom merge strategy for arrays versus scalars.
Resume-safe, single-writer, per-file commit concurrency over a thread pool.
Hybrid pipeline combining code-derived metadata, model-derived projections, and per-call telemetry.
conventions: Uses module-level global constants loaded at import time.
Mixes pure helper functions with imperative database writes.
Employs ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrency while serializing writes in the main loop.
Idempotent CREATE TABLE / ALTER TABLE migration in the entry point.
Hardcoded paths and substring-based heuristic classification.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute ROOT path limits portability across environments.
is_light relies on substring matching that can misclassify unrelated form names.
merge selects the longest scalar value, which may not be the most accurate.
Model/chunk failures are recorded but not retried, and all-chunks-failed leaves an empty projection.
CALL_LOG insertion uses the tag value for the rel column, which can be empty or misleading.
_old_write is dead code retained in the file.
ON CONFLICT update does not refresh reach, surface_claims, or form_specific columns.
notes: A resume-safe, chunked, schema-constrained capture worker that turns project files into a queryable SQLite ledger while logging every model call.
questions: What is the exact contract of fleet.local4b and where is the model endpoint configured?
What semantic meaning is intended for each projection field such as reach, surface_claims, and form_specific?
How is form_survey.jsonl generated and what schema does it follow?
Why was max-length chosen as the scalar merge strategy rather than first, last, or voting?
What is the structure of carve.json and how are member_rels selected?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended as a resume-safe capture worker that renders structured projections from files; the implementation matches that purpose, adding chunked rendering, light-form skipping, and per-file persistence.
intention_signals: It is trying to build a durable, incremental pipeline that converts raw project files into a queryable ledger of semantic projections, avoiding re-work and preserving model-call telemetry.
novelty: The combination of chunked model rendering with overlap, a light-form bypass to reduce model calls, and a single-writer-per-file commit inside a thread pool is distinctive.
contribution: A concrete worker implementation for incremental, chunked, schema-constrained LLM extraction of file semantics into an SQLite ledger, including code-derived links, blocks, lineage, and per-call logging.
summary_for_embedding: capture2.py is a resume-safe batch capture worker that renders JSON-schema-constrained legibility projections from project files using a local model, chunks large files with overlap, merges results, and persists files, projections, links, blocks, and call telemetry into SQLite.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to incrementally populate a structured ledger of file semantics and provenance from a design-code substrate without reprocessing already-captured files.

symbols.does: Constructs a JSON Schema object from the projection registry.
symbols.description: Reads MODEL_PROJ and maps each field type (array, enum, string) into a JSON Schema object with all fields required; this schema is passed to the model to constrain output.

symbols.does: Loads form classifications from a JSONL file.
symbols.description: Reads form_survey.jsonl, indexes each rel to its content_form, and skips malformed or error records; the resulting map drives the light-form decision.

symbols.does: Heuristic classifier for low-semantic file forms.
symbols.description: Returns true when the form string contains log, status, chronicle, index, or moc; light files receive code-only metadata and bypass model rendering.

symbols.does: Calls a local LLM with a JSON schema to render projections.
symbols.description: Builds a system-plus-user message from the system prompt, file path, frontmatter context, and body, invokes fleet.local4b with json_schema, and parses the returned JSON text.

symbols.does: Combines projection outputs from multiple chunks.
symbols.description: Merges a list of projection dictionaries by deduplicating array items and selecting the longest string value for scalar fields.

symbols.does: Captures metadata and projections for a single file.
symbols.description: Lifts code metadata via lift.lift, classifies the file as substance or light, reads the full text, chunks it if necessary, calls model_render per chunk, and returns a record including projections, errors, and chunk count.

symbols.does: Persists one capture record to SQLite.
symbols.description: Inserts or updates the files row, writes model and code projections, links, blocks, and flushes fleet call-log entries in a single commit.
Determines already-captured rels, submits the remainder to a thread pool, writes each completed result sequentially, commits per file, and reports progress.
Older implementation that writes a list of records in one pass and flushes the call log at the end; it remains in the file but is not invoked.
